reasoning: The Polymarket base rate from late October was 0.58 for 'no change' at the March 2026 meeting. Current market commentary from late 2025 (e.g., Mackenzie Investments in Nov 2025) suggests the Fed will likely end 2025 at around 3.6% and will only cut to around 3.4% by the end of 2026. This implies only one or possibly zero 25 bps cuts over the entire year of 2026. The probability of any given meeting being a 'pause' is quite high under this trajectory. However, because March is an SEP (Summary of Economic Projections) meeting, it is a naturally focal point for central bank action, meaning the likelihood of a cut occuring in March rather than Jan/May is elevated. Consequently, the market prices a relatively substantial (~40-42%) chance of a rate cut occurring at this meeting, leaving the probability of 'no change' around 58-60%. Given that the overall pace of cuts has slowed down and expectations for 2026 cuts have been continuously trimmed, a 'no change' outcome remains the slight favorite. I am predicting 59%, keeping close to the strong market anchor from late October.
